title: Stakeholders, Ethics, Accessibility, and Harm Analysis · Stakeholder Case: Stakeholder Power Map
summary: Primary-user convenience may shift surveillance, labor, exclusion, safety, environmental, or procedural costs onto less visible people. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone stakeholder power map increment in stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.
Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Stakeholder Power Map · Theory Stakeholder Power Map
steps.instruction:
theory case — Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Stakeholder Power Map contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Stakeholder Power Map · Theory Stakeholder Power Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users. For Predict Stakeholder Power Map · Theory Stakeholder Power Map, keep Stakeholder Power Map connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Stakeholder Power Map condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing

For Predict Stakeholder Power Map · Theory Stakeholder Power Map, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Stakeholder importance is proportional to purchasing power.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The worked example variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Map benefit, burden, exposure, voice, authority, recourse, and accountability across users and affected non-users. A defensible solution applies Stakeholder Power Map, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-3
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
